# Research — 2026-06-01

Focus: **all** (CLI, providers, tools, MCP, commands, hooks, skills, plugins, agents)

> Continuation of 2026-05-30. The 05-30 pass enumerated six new gaps (per-dir
> AGENTS.md as `<system-reminder>`, DOCX/XLSX in `read`, plugin auto-load +
> `alexi plugin init` scaffolder, default-agent in user config, OSC-8 clickable
> URLs, `reloadSkills` hook + `/reload-skills` command). **All six are now in
> `src/`.** This pass integrates claude-code **v2.1.158/v2.1.159** (May 30–31),
> kilocode **v7.3.18** wind-down (May 29 → no further user-facing CLI commits
> in the 7-day window beyond changelog/nix/`@ai-sdk/openai` syncs and Opus 4.8
> adaptive-thinking treatment), cline **v3.0.15 + #11166** (May 29 → Jun 1),
> aider (no functional code in window — model-list expansion + bash
> tree-sitter tags only), and continuedev (mercury-2 model registration,
> dynamic provider model fetching, OpenRouter header refactor, session-history
> workspace filter — most out of envelope; one item carries over below).

## Executive Summary

- **All 6 carry-overs from 2026-05-30 are closed.** Verified in code:
  per-directory AGENTS.md walks the path on `read` and emits
  `metadata.systemReminders` flushed by `agenticChat.ts:649`
  (`src/agent/system.ts:274` `instructionsForPath` + `src/tool/tools/read.ts:18,64-71`
  + `src/tool/index.ts:34-58` + tests in `src/tool/tools/__tests__/read.agentsmd.test.ts`);
  DOCX + XLSX extraction shipped as a dedicated module
  (`src/tool/tools/read-office.ts`, dispatched from `read.ts:190-199`);
  plugin auto-load lives in `src/plugin/index.ts:284-306,922-923,1294-1295`
  with `alexi plugin init <name>` in `src/cli/commands/plugin.ts`;
  default-agent honored across sessions via `resolveDefaultAgent`
  (`src/cli/commands/agent.ts:21,221`, `src/cli/commands/chat.ts:9,63-70`,
  `src/agent/defaultAgent.ts`); OSC-8 hyperlink helper in
  `src/cli/tui/utils/hyperlink.ts` + tests + used in `src/cli/interactive.ts:1181`;
  `SessionStart` hook output `reloadSkills: true` is parsed and dispatched
  (`src/hooks/index.ts:83,112,555-562`) plus a `/reload-skills` command
  (`src/command/reloadSkills.ts`) and tests
  (`src/hooks/__tests__/reload-skills.test.ts`).
- **claude-code v2.1.158 (May 30)**: auto-mode now on Bedrock/Vertex/Foundry
  behind `CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_AUTO_MODE=1`. Out of envelope (we route through
  SAP AI Core, no exposed safety classifier). No action.
- **claude-code v2.1.159 (May 31)**: internal-only. No action.
- **kilocode CLI 7.3.18 → 7.3.19-rc** (May 29 → Jun 1) is dominated by
  changelog backfills, nix node_modules-hash regen, and **Opus 4.8 adaptive
  thinking** treatment (`feat(cli): treat opus 4.8 as adaptive thinking model
  like 4.7`, #10735, merged Jun 1). Our `src/providers/` does not have an
  explicit "adaptive thinking" model list and never gated thinking output by
  model id in the first place, so no parity action is needed beyond making
  sure Opus 4.8 (`anthropic--claude-4-8-opus`/SAP's eventual id) is in the
  default model list when SAP exposes it. Tracker item only.
- **cline #11166 (Jun 1) — `fix: keep file search fallback alive`**: when
  the bundled-ripgrep path is missing or the host RPC is unavailable, cline
  now falls through to system `rg`. Our `src/tool/tools/grep.ts` already uses
  `findFiles()` (a portable JS implementation, not bundled rg), so we don't
  share the regression. **However** we have no system-`rg` fast path at all —
  large repos pay the JS-walker cost on every grep. Adding an
  optional-but-preferred system-rg dispatch (when `rg` is on `PATH`) is a
  worthwhile small win, independent of the cline bug.
- **cline #11127 (May 29) — plugin rule contributions in sandbox**: plugins
  can register static or dynamic *rule content* into the sandbox handler
  state. Our plugins can register commands/skills/MCP servers but **not**
  rules — there is no "rule" surface analogous to AGENTS.md content that a
  plugin can contribute. Worth designing now while the plugin loader is still
  small.
- **continuedev #11813 (in-window) — `workspaceDirectory` filter on session
  history listing**: we list sessions globally
  (`src/core/sessionManager.ts:188`); `SessionMetadata`
  (`src/core/sessionManager.ts:22-30`) does not record the workdir at all.
  Sessions from unrelated projects pile up in `alexi sessions`. Adding
  `workdir` to `SessionMetadata` and an optional filter to `listSessions()`
  is small and obviously correct.
- **claude-code v2.1.154 (May 28) recap — stdio MCP servers now receive
  `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` and `CLAUDECODE=1` env vars**: we already inject
  `ALEXI_PROJECT_DIR` before user `env` (`src/mcp/client.ts:170-175`). Adding
  `ALEXI_SESSION_ID` and `ALEXICODE=1` (or `ALEXI=1`) is a one-line change
  that lets MCP servers tell when they're invoked from Alexi vs. directly,
  and lets them tag logs/metrics by session. Trivial, no code-organisation
  cost.
- **Plugin marketplace `defaultEnabled: false` (claude-code v2.1.154)**: we
  have `pluginState.enabled` (`src/plugin/index.ts:1031-1119`) but no
  manifest-level default. Defer until we publish a marketplace; on-demand
  activation today is fine.
- **Aider (May 27 – Jun 1)**: model-list expansion only (Opus 4.5/4.6/4.7
  variants, Sonnet 4.6) — purely Python provider config. No parity action.
- **Continue (May 27 – Jun 1)**: dynamic provider model fetching, mercury-2
  registration, OpenRouter header refactor — all upstream-product changes
  with no Alexi parity.

## Detailed Findings

### What was closed since 2026-05-30 (verified in code)
- **Per-directory AGENTS.md as `<system-reminder>`** —
  `src/agent/system.ts:274` `instructionsForPath(absPath, workdir, seen)`
  walks from `path.dirname(absPath)` upward to `workdir`, returns
  nearest-first, excludes the project-root file, dedups via `realpathSync`
  (cycle/symlink-safe), and is bounded against deeply nested trees.
  `src/tool/tools/read.ts:64-71` calls it on each successful read and stuffs
  the results into `result.metadata.systemReminders`.
  `src/core/agenticChat.ts:649` flushes pending reminders into the next user
  message. Tests:
  `src/tool/tools/__tests__/read.agentsmd.test.ts:23-80`,
  `src/agent/system.test.ts:305-454` (covers nearest-first, root-exclusion,
  per-session de-dup, outside-workdir guard, symlink dedup, deep-nesting cap).
  Still **only `read.ts` participates** — `multiedit`/`edit`/`write`/`grep`
  do not yet emit system reminders for files they touch. See item #1 below.
- **DOCX + XLSX in `read`** — `src/tool/tools/read-office.ts:36-145` with
  `isOfficeDocument(filePath)` returning `'docx' | 'xlsx' | null`, `mammoth`
  and `xlsx` lazy-imported, sheet-by-sheet CSV preview. Dispatched from
  `read.ts:17,190-199`. `.docm` is intentionally excluded (mammoth doesn't
  cover macros).
- **Plugin auto-load + `alexi plugin init` scaffolder** —
  `src/plugin/index.ts:284-306,922-923,1294-1295` discovers plugin roots at
  `.alexi/skills/<name>/plugin.json` (project) and
  `~/.alexi/skills/<name>/plugin.json` (global). `src/cli/commands/plugin.ts`
  exposes `alexi plugin init <name>` with `--global` and `--description`,
  refuses to overwrite existing dirs, sanitises slugs, and emits
  `plugin.json` + `commands/<slug>.md` + `README.md`. README links to
  `/reload-skills`.
- **Default-agent in user config** —
  `src/agent/defaultAgent.ts` (`resolveDefaultAgent`),
  `src/config/userConfig.ts:188` reads top-level `agent` field,
  `src/cli/commands/agent.ts:21-81,221` and `src/cli/commands/chat.ts:9,36-70`
  apply `--agent` flag → config `agent` field → undefined precedence.
- **OSC-8 hyperlinks** — `src/cli/tui/utils/hyperlink.ts` with TERM_PROGRAM
  allow-list detection, `supports-hyperlinks`-style env probing, `hyperlink(url, label?)`
  with graceful plain-text and `"label (url)"` fallbacks. Tests in
  `src/cli/tui/utils/hyperlink.test.ts`. Used in `src/cli/interactive.ts:1181,1184`.
- **`reloadSkills` hook + `/reload-skills` command** —
  `src/hooks/index.ts:83,112` (schema), `:555-562` (only fires on
  `SessionStart`, only once per turn even if multiple hooks ask),
  `src/command/reloadSkills.ts`, tests
  `src/hooks/__tests__/reload-skills.test.ts:74-163`.

### Still open from 2026-05-30
- None. (5 of 5 small wins shipped + Item #3's medium-effort plugin work
  shipped + Item #1's per-dir AGENTS.md shipped for `read` only — see
  carry-over below.)

### New gaps (sorted by leverage)

#### 1. Extend per-directory AGENTS.md system-reminders to write/edit/multiedit/grep (Category: tool — small, < 4h)
- **What**: The `instructionsForPath` plumbing already handles the hard
  parts (path walking, realpath dedup, per-session `seen` set, deep-nesting
  cap). Today only `src/tool/tools/read.ts` calls it. When the agent edits
  `apps/api/src/foo.ts` via `edit` or `multiedit`, the `apps/api/AGENTS.md`
  rules don't get injected unless the agent happened to `read` the file
  first. Wire the same call site into `edit.ts`, `multiedit.ts`, `write.ts`,
  and (lower priority) `grep.ts` matched files.
- **Why**: This is the obvious follow-on to the 05-30 ship. Without it the
  per-dir AGENTS.md story is leaky for the most common monorepo workflow
  ("agent makes a quick edit without reading first"). The infrastructure
  is identical to `read.ts`'s integration — same `context.agentsMdSeen` is
  already on the tool context.
- **Source**: design follow-through on the 05-30 implementation; kilocode
  #10707 documents this exact "applies to file-touching tools" expectation.
- **Size**: small (< 4h). Three to four call-site additions, four to five
  test additions mirroring `read.agentsmd.test.ts`.
- **Implementation sketch**:
  - In each of `edit.ts`, `multiedit.ts`, `write.ts`: after a successful
    write, call `instructionsForPath(absPath, context.workdir, context.agentsMdSeen)`
    and append to `result.metadata.systemReminders` (same shape as `read.ts`).
  - In `grep.ts`: when results contain matches under subtrees with
    AGENTS.md, emit one reminder per *unique* AGENTS.md whose subtree
    contains any match. Bounded by `seen` so we don't spam.
  - Test pattern: tmp workdir, write `apps/api/AGENTS.md`, invoke the tool
    on `apps/api/src/foo.ts`, assert one reminder; second call should not
    re-emit.

#### 2. Inject `ALEXI_SESSION_ID` and `ALEXICODE=1` into stdio MCP server env (Category: mcp — small, < 1h)
- **What**: `src/mcp/client.ts:170-175` already injects `ALEXI_PROJECT_DIR`
  before user-supplied `env`. Add `ALEXI_SESSION_ID` (set to the active
  session id from `sessionManager.getActiveSession()?.metadata.id`) and
  `ALEXICODE=1` so MCP servers can detect Alexi-driven invocations.
- **Why**: claude-code v2.1.154 (May 28) shipped exactly this pattern
  (`CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID`, `CLAUDECODE=1`). It costs us ~5 lines and
  unlocks two real use cases: (a) MCP servers tagging telemetry by session,
  (b) MCP servers behaving differently when invoked outside an Alexi
  session (e.g. via `npx @user/mcp-foo` from a shell). Today MCP servers
  have no signal that they're inside Alexi.
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code v2.1.154 release notes — "Stdio MCP
  server subprocesses now receive `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` and
  `CLAUDECODE=1` in their environment".
- **Size**: small (< 1h).
- **Implementation sketch**:
  - Thread the active session id into `connectStdio()` via the existing
    `McpConnectOptions` (which already carries `workdir`).
  - Insert into the `env` object literal at `src/mcp/client.ts:171`:
    ```ts
    ALEXI_PROJECT_DIR: options?.workdir ?? process.cwd(),
    ALEXI_SESSION_ID: options?.sessionId ?? '',
    ALEXICODE: '1',
    ```
  - Test: spawn a tiny stdio MCP echo-server fixture and assert env passes
    through after the user-config `env` merge.

#### 3. `workdir` on `SessionMetadata` + optional `workdir` filter on `listSessions()` (Category: config — small, < 4h)
- **What**: Add an optional `workdir?: string` to `SessionMetadata`
  (`src/core/sessionManager.ts:22-30`). On session creation, record
  `process.cwd()`. `listSessions(opts?: { workdir?: string })` filters by
  exact match (case-insensitive) when set. `alexi sessions` grows a
  `--workdir` / `--here` flag that defaults to filtering by `process.cwd()`,
  with `--all` to disable.
- **Why**: continuedev #11813 (in-window) shipped this and the rationale
  applies one-for-one: a developer working across many repos accumulates
  hundreds of sessions, and `alexi sessions` becomes a wall of noise. Today
  we have no way to scope. The data is already there at session-creation
  time; we just don't persist it.
- **Source**: continuedev/continue#11813 — "filter session history by
  workspace directory" (in-window).
- **Size**: small (< 4h). Schema migration is forgiving — the field is
  optional, missing-field sessions just don't filter (or fall through to
  `--all`).
- **Implementation sketch**:
  - Add `workdir?: string` to `SessionMetadata`. Backfill is unnecessary;
    pre-existing sessions have `workdir === undefined` and the filter
    treats them as "no opinion" (excluded from `--here`, included in
    `--all`).
  - Set in `createSession()` next to `created`/`updated`.
  - Add filter to `listSessions(opts?: { workdir?: string })` —
    case-insensitive `path.resolve` compare.
  - `src/cli/commands/sessions.ts`: add `--here` / `--workdir <dir>` /
    `--all` flags. Default behaviour: keep current (list everything) unless
    a flag is set, to avoid breaking existing scripts.

#### 4. Plugin "rule contributions" surface (Category: plugin — medium, 1 day)
- **What**: Allow a plugin's `plugin.json` to declare *rules* — markdown
  content (static or generated by a script) that gets injected into the
  agent's system instructions when the plugin is enabled. Mirror cline's
  `pluginRule` descriptor: `{ name, scope: 'session' | 'always',
  source: 'inline' | 'file' | 'command' }`. Inline ships content directly,
  file points at a markdown file under the plugin root, command runs a
  shell command and uses stdout (cached per session).
- **Why**: cline #11127 (May 29) shipped this as a sandbox-handler
  feature. The pattern matters for our plugin model too: today a plugin
  can ship commands and skills, but the *opinionated team-wide rules*
  ("always favour zod schemas over manual JSON parsing", "never log to
  stdout in workers") still have to live in `AGENTS.md`, which is owned by
  the repo, not by the plugin. Plugins that want to enforce guidelines
  have to ship a doc telling the user to copy-paste into AGENTS.md.
- **Source**: cline/cline#11127 — "feat(plugin): support rule contributions
  in sandbox", also tied to the cline plugin/sandbox descriptor refactor.
- **Size**: medium (1 day). Three sub-pieces:
  - (a) Schema: extend `PluginManifest` (`src/plugin/index.ts`) with a
    `rules?: PluginRule[]` field plus a Zod validator.
  - (b) Loader: when a plugin is enabled, read its rules and add them to
    the system-prompt assembly path in `src/agent/system.ts` after the
    AGENTS.md block (so AGENTS.md still wins on conflicts).
  - (c) `command` source: spawn the script with `cwd` = plugin root,
    bounded timeout (5s), bounded output (32 KB), cache result keyed by
    `(pluginId, scope === 'session' ? sessionId : 'always')`.
- **Implementation sketch**:
  ```jsonc
  // .alexi/skills/my-team-rules/plugin.json
  {
    "name": "my-team-rules",
    "version": "0.1.0",
    "rules": [
      { "name": "core-style", "source": "file", "path": "rules/core-style.md" },
      { "name": "current-todos", "source": "command", "command": "./scripts/today.sh", "scope": "session" }
    ]
  }
  ```

#### 5. System-`rg` fast-path for `grep`/`codesearch` when available (Category: tool — small, < 4h)
- **What**: When `rg` is on `PATH`, dispatch grep through it (sequential
  `--json` output → parse) instead of the JS file walker. Fall back to the
  current pure-JS `findFiles()` path when `rg` is missing or the spawn fails.
  Same applies to `codesearch.ts`'s `findCodeFiles()` for the file-listing
  half.
- **Why**: cline #11166 (Jun 1) reminded us that ripgrep is the standard
  fast path; their fix was to *retain* the system-rg fallback when the
  bundled binary is missing. We never had a system-rg path at all, so on
  large repos every grep does a JS recursive walk. Even without bundled rg
  we can opportunistically use it. Performance win on monorepos is large
  (single-digit-ms vs hundred-of-ms for >10k file repos).
- **Source**: cline/cline#11166 (Jun 1) and the long-standing pattern of
  preferring rg in agentic tools (kilocode's `grep` and claude-code's
  `Grep` both wrap rg by default).
- **Size**: small (< 4h). One spawn helper + a feature-detect on first use.
- **Implementation sketch**:
  - In `src/tool/tools/grep.ts`: at module init (lazy), check
    `which rg` (or attempt a `--version` spawn with 200ms timeout). Cache
    result.
  - When available: spawn `rg --json --no-heading --line-number <pattern>
    <path>` with `include` translated to `--glob`. Stream stdout, parse
    JSON-lines, build the same `ToolResult.matches` shape.
  - When not available, fall through to current path (no behavioural
    change for environments without rg).
  - Add an env override `ALEXI_DISABLE_RG=1` for users who hit
    rg-version-incompatibility issues. Document.

### Notable upstream items deliberately deferred
- **Auto-mode on Bedrock/Vertex/Foundry (claude-code v2.1.158)**: SAP AI
  Core does not yet expose an equivalent classifier. Re-evaluate when it
  does.
- **`tool_decision`/`tool_parameters` OTEL events (v2.1.157, recap)**: no
  OTEL instrumentation in `src/`. Defer until there's a downstream consumer.
- **`MessageDisplay` hook (v2.1.152, recap)**: still no demand. Defer.
- **`claude agents` PR column / `! <cmd>` background sessions (v2.1.153–157)**:
  depend on a background-agent infrastructure we don't have.
- **Dynamic workflows + Opus 4.8 default-high-effort (v2.1.154+)**: upstream
  product orchestration, not a CLI parity item.
- **Plugin marketplace `defaultEnabled: false` (v2.1.154)**: only relevant
  once we publish a marketplace.
- **Cline Hub web app (#10969)**: out of envelope (web product, not CLI).
- **Cline Discord connector (#11121, #11126, #11127's Discord half)**: out
  of envelope.
- **continuedev mercury-2 registration / dynamic-model-fetching (#12046,
  #12156)**: model registry differences — SAP exposes a different surface.
  No action.
- **continuedev OpenRouter header refactor (#11991)**: we're not an
  OpenRouter consumer.
- **aider model-list expansion + bash tree-sitter tags**: Python-only.
- **kilocode #10735 Opus 4.8 adaptive-thinking treatment**: we don't gate
  on adaptive-thinking model lists today; no action until SAP's Opus 4.8
  id is finalised, then add to the model registry.
- **POSIX `cd`/`pushd`/subshell permission audit (deferred from 2026-05-29)**:
  still on the to-do list. Open a tracking issue if not yet filed.

## Actionable Recommendations

1. **Ship the small wins as one cycle (~half a day total)**:
   - **Item #2** (`ALEXI_SESSION_ID` / `ALEXICODE` env in MCP stdio): one-line
     env addition + plumbing of `sessionId` through `McpConnectOptions`.
   - **Item #3** (`workdir` on `SessionMetadata` + filter): self-contained,
     small migration story (optional field).
   - **Item #5** (system-`rg` fast-path): biggest user-visible perf win, but
     keep it simple — feature-detect, dispatch, fall back.
   These three land as separate PRs and don't conflict (different
   directories). Total effort ~1 day with tests.

2. **Item #1 (extend per-dir AGENTS.md to write/edit/multiedit/grep)** is
   the obvious follow-through on the 05-30 ship. It's the smallest of the
   set and finishes the story for monorepo edits. Pair with Item #2 in the
   same PR cycle since neither touches the orchestrator deeply.

3. **Item #4 (plugin rule contributions)** is the medium-effort feature
   for this cycle. Land in two PRs:
   - (a) Schema + `inline` and `file` sources only (small).
   - (b) `command` source with timeout/size bounds + caching (small).
   Sequence so (a) ships first and (b) follows once we see real use.

4. **Open the deferred POSIX `cd`/`pushd` permission audit issue** if it
   wasn't filed in the prior cycles — it's still on the carry-over list
   from 2026-05-29 and is not getting smaller.

5. **Track Opus 4.8 SAP AI Core availability**: when SAP exposes the model,
   add the id to `src/providers/` model lists. No code change is needed
   today (we don't gate on adaptive-thinking model id strings), but it's
   worth a calendar note.

6. **Tracker hygiene**: the 2026-05-30 entry's six "new gaps" are now zero.
   Velocity signal is strong — surface that in the next planning doc.

7. **Re-evaluate next cycle**: SAP-AI-Core auto-mode classifier
   availability, OTEL instrumentation demand, plugin-marketplace timeline
   (which would unlock `defaultEnabled: false` and `skipLfs`).
